segments.text: Good morning everyone.
segments.translation: 大家早上好.

segments.text: My name's Janet Parker and I'm the human resources manager.
segments.translation: 我叫珍妮特·帕克，是人力资源部经理。

segments.text: We're very happy to welcome you to your new apprenticeship.
segments.translation: 我们非常欢迎你们加入学徒期。

segments.text: I hope that the next six months will be a positive and enjoyable experience for you.
segments.translation: 我希望接下来的六个月对你们来说是一次积极愉快的经历。

segments.text: I'd like to start with some general advice about being an apprentice.
segments.translation: 首先我来说一下对学徒们的一些建议。

segments.text: Most of you have very little or no experience of working for a big organisation and the first week or so may be quite challenging.
segments.translation: 你们中的大多数人只有很少或完全没有在大公司工作的经验，因此第一周对于你们来说可能是相当具有挑战性的。

segments.text: There will be a lot of new information to take in but don't worry too much about trying to remember everything.
segments.translation: 你们将会消化很多新信息，但是不用太担心自己记不住所有的事情。

segments.text: The important thing is to check with someone if you're not sure what to do - you'll find your supervisor is very approachable and won't mind explaining things or helping you out.
segments.translation: 重要的是，如果你们不确定该做什么，就主动询问，你们会发现你们的主管很平易近人，他们不会介意为你们解释或提供帮助。

segments.text: You're here to learn so make the most of that opportunity.
segments.translation: 你们是来学习的，所以要好好利用这个机会。

segments.text: You'll be spending time in different departments during your first week so make an effort to talk to as many people as possible about their work - you'll make some new friends and find out lots of useful information.
segments.translation: 在第一周，你们会在不同的部门呆上一段时间，所以要努力和尽可能多的人谈论他们的工作——你们会结交一些新朋友，并且会找到很多有用的信息。

segments.text: As well as having a supervisor, you'll each be assigned a mentor.
segments.translation: 除了一位主管外，你们每人还将被分配一位导师。

segments.text: This person will be someone who's recently completed an apprenticeship and you'll meet with them on a weekly basis.
segments.translation: 导师们将是最近刚结束学徒期的人，你们将每周与他们见面。

segments.text: Their role is to provide help and support throughout your apprenticeship.
segments.translation: 他们负责在你们的整个学徒期提供帮助和支持。

segments.text: Of course, this doesn't mean they actually do any of your work for you - instead they'll be asking you about what goals you've achieved so far, as well as helping you to identify any areas for improvement.
segments.translation: 当然，这并不意味着他们会为你做任何具体的工作，他们会问你们到目前为止你实现了什么目标，以及帮助你们明确任何需要改进的领域。

segments.text: You can also discuss your more long-term ambitions with them as well.
segments.translation: 你们也可以和他们讨论自己更长远的抱负。

segments.text: Now I just want to run through a few company policies for our apprenticeship scheme with you...
segments.translation: 现在我要你们讲一遍公司学徒计划的一些相关政策……

segments.text: Most importantly, the internet.
segments.translation: 最重要的是，公司网络。

segments.text: As part of your job you'll be doing some research online so obviously you'll have unlimited access for that but please don't use it for personal use - you'll have your own phones for that.
segments.translation: 你们将在网上做一些调查，这是你们工作的一部分，所以很明显，你们可以无限制地使用网络，但是请不要将它用于个人用途，你们可以使用自己的手机上网。

segments.text: Some of you have already asked me about flexible working.
segments.translation: 你们中有些人已经问过我关于弹性工作的问题了。

segments.text: After your probationary three-month period - some of you will be eligible for this - but it will depend on which department you're in and what your personal circumstances are.
segments.translation: 在三个月的试用期结束后——你们中的一些人将有弹性工作的资格--但这将取决于你们所在的部门和你们的个人情况。

segments.text: So please don't assume you'll automatically be permitted to do this.
segments.translation: 所以请不要认为你们会自动被允许采用弹性工作制。

segments.text: I want to make sure there's no confusion about our holiday policy.
segments.translation: 我想确保你们对我们的假期政策没有疑惑。

segments.text: Apart from any statutory public holidays we ask that you don't book any holidays until after your six-month apprenticeship has finished.
segments.translation: 除任何法定公众假期外，我们要求你们在完成六个月的学徒期前，不要预订任何假期。

segments.text: Time off should only be taken if you are unwell.
segments.translation: 只有当你们身体不舒服的时候，才可以请假。

segments.text: Please speak to your supervisor if this is going to be a problem.
segments.translation: 如果这一点对你来说有问题，请向你的主管咨询。

segments.text: You'll be expected to work a 40-hour week but there may be opportunities to do overtime during busy periods.
segments.translation: 你们每周需要工作40小时，但是在繁忙时期会有加班的可能性。

segments.text: Although you're not required to do this, it can be a valuable experience - so we advise you to take it up if possible.
segments.translation: 虽然加班不是硬性要求，但这是一次宝贵的经历。所以我们建议你们尽可能地接受。

segments.text: Obviously, we understand that people do have commitments outside work, so don't worry if there are times when you are unavailable.
segments.translation: 当然，我们理解人们在工作之外确实有脱不开身的事情，所以如果有时你们没有办法加班，也不要担心。

segments.text: As you know, we don't have a formal dress code here - you may wear casual clothes as long as they're practical - and the only restriction for shoes we have is on high heels for health and safety reasons.
segments.translation: 正如你所知道的，我们这里没有正式的着装规定——你可以穿休闲的衣服，只要它们是实用的——我们对鞋子的唯一限制是不能穿高跟鞋，这是为了员工的健康和安全。

segments.text: Comfortable shoes like trainers are preferable.
segments.translation: 像运动鞋这样舒适的鞋比较好。

segments.text: There's a heavily subsidised canteen on site where you can get hot meals or salads cheaply.
segments.translation: 这里有一家由公司补贴的职工食堂，你们可以买到便宜的热餐或沙拉。

segments.text: Snacks and drinks are also provided - so we've decided to introduce a no packed lunch policy.
segments.translation: 小吃和饮料也有供应——所以我们决定实行禁止自带午餐的政策。

segments.text: This is partly to encourage healthy eating at work and partly to stop people from eating at their workstation, which is unhygienic.
segments.translation: 这一方面是为了鼓励人们在工作时健康饮食，另一方面是为了阻止人们在工位吃东西的不卫生行为。

segments.text: OK moving on to…
segments.translation: 好的，我们继续谈谈……
